stopConditions: Stop after verified official sources cover student assessment guidance, information governance, academic-integrity policy, and staff guidance.
Do not use resource hubs, course listings, articles, or search snippets as evidence.

artifacts.title: University of St Andrews AI Policy Tracker Report - May 2026
artifacts.summary: This staged run found official St Andrews sources covering student-facing generative AI assessment guidance, Good Academic Practice treatment of unauthorised AI use, information-governance restrictions for public GenAI tools, procurement routing for GenAI tools, Copilot availability, and staff guidance on AI detection. The run rejected official resource and workshop pages as lower-specificity context rather than policy evidence.
limitations: Claims are staged agent-reviewed candidates only and are not canonical public policy conclusions.
The CELPiE AI professional development resource hub and PDMS workshop page were rejected as non-policy evidence.
No login-gated, paywalled, CAPTCHA-protected, or WAF-blocked source was used as evidence.
This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Official source page retains its original rights. Tracker metadata is open licensed; source text is quoted only as short evidence.
